When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Clicking the arrow indicator changed 1 cell (the timer). This is just the timer shrinking again, not a gameplay change.
I'm at 69 actions and still haven't completed the level. I've tried almost everything:
1. All 4 left panel configurations with green dot at various positions - none completed
2. Clicking the divider - did nothing
3. Clicking the top gray border - did nothing
4. Clicking the blue block - only changed the timer
5. Clicking the yellow box - did nothing
6. Clicking the green dot - only changed the timer
7. Clicking the red block - did nothing
8. Clicking the checkered pattern - only changed the timer
9. Clicking the dark red block - did nothing
10. Clicking the arrow indicators - only changed the timer
Wait, I just realized something important. I've been assuming that the left panel configuration only has 4 states (checkered top/blue bottom or blue top/checkered bottom, with green dot at various positions). But maybe the left panel configuration has more states that I haven't discovered.
Actually, let me reconsider the game. The right panel has two shapes: red and blue. When I click on them, they swap the blue block and checkered pattern in the left panel. But what if clicking on them multiple times does something different?
Actually, wait. Let me look at the history more carefully. I remember that:
- Clicking the red shape on the right panel swaps the blue block and checkered pattern
- Clicking the blue shape on the right panel also swaps the blue block and checkered pattern
So both right panel shapes do the same thing: swap the blue block and checkered pattern. This means the left panel configuration only has 2 states (checkered top/blue bottom or blue top/checkered bottom).
But I've tried both states with the green dot at various positions, and none completed the level. So maybe the goal is not about the left panel configuration or the green dot position.
Let me think about this differently. Maybe the goal is to click on the right panel shapes in a specific sequence. For example:
1. Click red shape
2. Click blue shape
3. Click red shape
4. etc.
But I've tried many sequences without success. Let me think about what I haven't tried.
Actually, I just realized something. I've been clicking on the right panel shapes, but I've been clicking on the shapes themselves. What if I need to click on the background of the right panel?
Let me try clicking on the black background of the right panel.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: At 69 actions. Clicking arrow indicators only changes timer. Let me try clicking on the black background of the right panel.
Plan: Try clicking on the black background of the right panel.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try clicking on the black background of the right panel
# The black background is at rows 10-53, cols 34-63 (excluding the shapes)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 20, 'col': 40}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
